description_long: This is a two-story wooden house with a prominent gable roof covered in brown shingles and a central brick chimney. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, with a brick foundation running along the base. The front facade features a small covered porch with a gabled roof, supported by simple posts, and a set of steps leading up to the main entrance. Large rectangular window openings are present on both floors, with the lower right window slightly larger than the others. The roof extends over the sides, providing deep eaves, and the overall design is simple and functional, typical of suburban or rural American homes.
description: A two-story wooden house with a gable roof, brick foundation, and a small covered front porch. Large rectangular windows and a central chimney add to its classic suburban appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a gabled porch, steps, and two large window openings on each floor.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the full expanse of the shingled roof, chimney, and a plain wall with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the extended gable roof, plain wooden siding, and the chimney rising from the roof ridge.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the uninterrupted wooden siding and the depth of the roof overhang.
attribute_entryway_design: Small gabled porch with steps, simple posts, and a recessed main door.
